objective: Compile a card-material-profile JSON plus supplied mask assets into deterministic browser delivery assets (generated/cards/<profile-id>/: card.css, card-manifest.json, assets/) consumed by HoloCard.svelte, for grid and detail tiers plus static/reduced-motion fallbacks and a WebGL handoff manifest stub. The compiler NEVER creates or infers material maps and NEVER contains card-specific constants. Issue #9.
current_state: No CSS profile compiler exists. schemas/card-material-profile.schema.json defines profiles (incl. lane + reference labels). HoloCard.svelte/CardProxy.svelte and public/css/cards/ carry the upstream pointer/spring/CSS-variable interaction boundary. Synthetic profile fixtures exist under examples/. Publication remains fail-closed for reference lane; internal-reference-prototype is a coded non-publishable state.
assumptions: Node-side compiler following the existing scripts/*.mjs + tests-node + tests-web wrapper conventions; wire a `compile:profile` npm script and, if a node test runner script is needed, `test:node` (package.json edits limited to scripts entries).
generated/ output is git-ignored; tests write to temp dirs; no generated artifact is ever committed (the generated-artifacts gate stays authoritative).
Synthetic fixtures only for this PR; output is never described as an accurate One Piece card.
acceptance_criteria: Deterministic: identical profile + asset hashes produce byte-identical card.css, card-manifest.json, and asset copies; manifest binds profile hash + per-asset hashes.

Six material layers routed independently (albedo, diffractive foil, metallic, texture/etching, clearcoat glare, ink suppression) into the documented CSS variable set (--foil-strength, --foil-band-angle, --foil-band-width, --foil-hue-offset, --metallic-strength, --metallic-warmth, --gloss-strength, --gloss-size, --texture-strength, --texture-scale, --texture-direction, --ink-suppression); no single generic rainbow overlay.

Pokemon-derived interaction boundary preserved: normalized pointer state, spring smoothing, CSS custom properties, stable card-layer markup, separate shine/glare layers, profile/proxy resolution; no Pokemon rarity assumptions or textures copied.

Tiers: grid output (CSS-only simplified effective masks, offscreen suspension, reduced-motion + static fallbacks) and detail output (independent multi-channel, restrained tilt); hero = WebGL handoff manifest JSON only when the profile declares channels CSS cannot reproduce (no GLB, no WebGL implementation).

State gating: compiles synthetic-fixture, internal-reference-prototype (output clearly marked private/non-publishable in manifest + CSS comment header), and approved production profiles; production compilation fails closed unless the full publication gate passes (invoke/require the existing gate result, do not reimplement it); any other state refuses.

Tests cover: determinism, hash binding, missing-channel static degradation, independent mask routing, internal-prototype non-public marker, production publication rejection, reduced-motion output, invalid/private asset URI rejection (private-media paths refused), and a source scan proving no card-specific constants in compiler source.

Visual regression: Playwright spec renders compiled synthetic output in HoloCard at poses neutral/left/right/top/bottom tilt/glare center/glare edge and compares against the research renderer at the same poses with a documented tolerance, recording that CSS is an approximation (docs/operations/css-profile-compiler.md).
required_tests: node --test tests-node/profile-compiler.test.mjs (or the wired npm script)
required_visual_checks: Pose grid contact sheet: compiled CSS vs research renderer at the seven canonical poses on a synthetic fixture
stop_conditions: Any requirement would need editing the profile schema, python pipeline, or agent gates (report, do not edit).
The publication gate cannot be consumed without reimplementing it (report the exact seam).
Any card-specific constant would be needed in compiler source.
expected_outputs: HoloCard/CardProxy wiring for compiled variables (minimal, boundary-preserving)
Synthetic fixture profiles + node/Playwright tests + visual comparison harness
